draft.introduction: Matthew 8:23–27 recounts the stilling of the storm on the Sea of Galilee. The disciples’ question—“What sort of man is this?”—frames the Christological inquiry of the entire Gospel. From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: And when he got into the boat, his disciples followed him. And behold, a great tempest arose on the sea, so that the boat was being swamped by the waves. But he was sleeping. And they came and woke him, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing.”
translation.original_text: και εμβαντι αυτω εις το πλοιον ηκολουθησαν αυτω οι μαθηται αυτου

translation.text: And he said to them, “Why are you afraid, you of little faith?” Then he rose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm. And the men marvelled, saying, “What sort of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?”
translation.original_text: και λεγει αυτοις τι δειλοι εστε ολιγοπιστοι

translator_notes.note: The question “What sort of man is this?” (potapos estin houtos) implies that Jesus transcends ordinary human categories. Command over wind and sea was an attribute of God alone in the Hebrew Bible (Psalm 107:29).
